demographics.occupation: works long hours (occupation not specified in source)

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: My heart keeps skipping a beat and then doing a massive, heavy thud that makes me catch my breath, and I'm terrified to fall asleep.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: It started tonight when I lay down in bed — it feels like my heart keeps stopping and thumping, and I'm scared something is really wrong with it.

symptom_narrative.onset: this evening, when I lay down in bed
symptom_narrative.duration: on and off through the evening
symptom_narrative.character: it feels like my heart skips a beat and then thumps really hard in my chest — a sudden heavy jolt
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: the thud makes me catch my breath and it's really frightening, but I'm not in pain
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: in the middle of my chest
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: comes in random single jolts, and I notice it much more when I'm lying quietly in bed
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: I haven't found anything that helps yet
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: it's worse when I lie down and try to sleep, and I've had a lot of coffee and been really run down
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: I get a brief light-headed feeling with the big thud, but I haven't fainted; no chest pain and I'm not short of breath sitting still
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: I don't remember it ever being this obvious before
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: I can't relax or fall asleep because I keep waiting for the next thud

social_history_volunteered.diet_and_exercise: drank a lot of coffee today
social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: has been working long hours, is very run down and fatigued, and lives alone which is making the worry worse

access_barriers.other: lives alone, presenting after hours, no one else at home to help
